title: Bevis of Hampton
summary: The General Corpus artifact is the pinned anz2316 XML, copied from the raw University of Michigan CME encoding after fully classified identifier, editorial-declaration, and whitespace changes. The U-M source package separately contains the raw XML and a complete 516-TIFF facsimile of Kölbing's complete 1885-1894 three-part edition. Kölbing presents one anonymous Middle English Bevis of Hampton through two alternative, non-additive editorial streams: A, based on Auchinleck with 176 missing lines supplied from Egerton S and broad collation; and x, based on Chetham M and Pynson O with de Worde L/O variants. Complete-codex NLS and Trinity IIIF bundles, parallel historical and modern editions, manuscript/print access, layer-specific rights, rejected source controls, and all unresolved locator, chronology, source-redaction, and licensing conflicts remain distinct.

review_notes: A and x are alternative editorial streams of one work and must never be summed as two works; title matter and apparatus are representation layers excluded from work extent.
Complete IIIF bundles preserve whole codices. Their work_portion locators identify Bevis surfaces without representing the cached ZIPs as cropped work-only downloads.

The Internet Archive raw U-M package is an aggregate wrapper containing two distinct layers: raw XML encoding and 516-TIFF facsimile. The same complete ZIP is intentionally projected on both package-download and TIFF-facsimile access.
The normalized copied_from assertion is limited to the exact transformations and canonical equality recorded in evidence:xml-normalization-audit; it does not claim byte identity.

Rights remain component-, jurisdiction-, access-, and provider-layer specific; medieval wording, historical edition text, modern editorial content, scans, XML, metadata, and package wrapper do not inherit one another's status.

The misleading Internet Archive item romanceofsirbeue00kluoft resolves to The Four Sons of Aymon rather than Bevis; its metadata and audited payload are retained as rejected-source evidence, not lineage ancestors.
